FURTHER HEARING IS SCHEDULED FOR JANUARY 25, 2018 AT 10 AM AT THE OFFICE OF PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL IS TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312 AND SUBMIT IT TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM FOR APPROVAL. JUDGE PRO TEM: MELINDA DERISH; CLERK: G. GONZALES; NOT REPORTED. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-05-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Cindy Cohn, Esq. (415-436-9333) of Electroninc Frontier Foundation and James wheaton, Esq. (415-601-5185) for Plaintiff.
Jonathan Doolittle, Esq. (415-659-502) of Reed Smith for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares his thoughts and concerns re: airline overbooking.
The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT BUDGET TRUCK RENTAL LLC's (ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS AVIS DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT). Defendant Budget Truck Rental, LLC's demurrer to the complaint is overruled as to the first, second and third causes of action and sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the fourth, fifth and sixth causes of action. Liberally construed, the complaint adequately alleges that Budget deliberately overbooked rentals for the type of truck requested by Dr. Ball knowing that it could not or would likely not be able to satisfy all the reservations it accepted for that type of truck and that Budget omitted to tell Dr. Ball that it deliberately overbooked rentals. This allegation is sufficient to state cognizable claims for violations of the CLRA (Civil Code 1770(a)(1)), all three prongs of the UCL, and Business and Professions Code 17500. Dr. Ball's claims for breach of contract and breach of the implied covenant are based on the allegation that he had a contract that obligated Budget to rent the truck to him. However, exhibit A to the complaint states that "[a] reservation is not a contract" and "[a]ll equipment selected in a reservation is subject to availability at time of pickup" contradicts and supersedes Dr. Ball's allegation of an enforceable contract obligating Budget to rent the truck to him. The quoted language also precludes the allegation for the promissory estoppel claim that Budge promised to rent the truck to Dr. Ball. If he can do so in good faith, Dr. Ball is granted leave to allege cognizable claims for breach of contract, breach of the implied covenant and promissory estoppel. (as fully reported on the reporter's notes.) Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 711, ckaren@sftc.org =(302/HK)
